args.searchTerm: t3-orchestrator cursor agent sdk proposed_plan record replay

references.title: Web search results
references.chunk:
Links:
1. [Cursor Python SDK | Cursor Documentation](https://cursor.com/docs/sdk/python)
2. [GustavoWinter/cursor-agent-orchestrator-mcp](https://github.com/GustavoWinter/cursor-agent-orchestrator-mcp)
3. [Build programmatic agents with the Cursor SDK · Cursor](https://cursor.com/blog/typescript-sdk)
4. [@cursor/february](https://registry.npmjs.org/@cursor/february)
5. [docs/development/cursor-backend.md at main · ralphkrauss/agent-orchestrator](https://github.com/ralphkrauss/agent-orchestrator/blob/main/docs/development/cursor-backend.md)

Synthesis:
To record and replay plans using the Cursor SDK, you utilize `mode="plan"` during agent creation or updates [1]. 

The Cursor SDK itself does not include a native `proposed_plan` record/replay mechanism; such functionality is typically implemented via an orchestrator layer [2]. Using the `@cursor/sdk` TypeScript or Python packages, you control this flow as follows [3][1]:

- Plan creation: Set `mode="plan"` in `Agent.create` or `agent.send` to trigger plan-oriented output [1].
- Record: You must manually record these events via the SDK's streaming interface (e.g., `run.stream()` in TypeScript or `on_delta`/`on_step` callbacks in Python) [4][1].
- Replay: Replaying requires consuming these stored event logs and re-initiating the agent state via `Agent.resume(agentId)` [5][4][1].

Third-party MCP orchestrators provide ready-made tools like `propose_plan` and `confirm_plan` to automate this [2]. These tools handle the persistence of plan states and approvals in an external JSON file, allowing you to gate execution until the plan is confirmed [2].

Highlights:

<title>Cursor Python SDK | Cursor Documentation</title>
<url>https://cursor.com/docs/sdk/python</url>
<content>The async client mirrors the
[...]
surface and is recommended for servers, bots, and

cursor_sdk
[...]
### Conversation mode
[...]
Pass `mode="plan"` or `mode="agent"` to control whether a run explores and plans first or implements changes directly. See Plan mode for what plan mode does in the product.
[...]
Set `mode` on `Agent.create()` to seed the first run. On follow-up `agent.send()` calls, omit `mode` to keep the conversation's current mode, or pass `mode` to switch for that run only.
[...]
) as agent:
[...]
| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `model` | `str | ModelSelection | Mapping[str, Any]` | Per-send model override. If omitted, uses `agent.model`. Sticky after a successful send. |
| `mode` | `"agent" | "plan"` | Per-send conversation mode override. If omitted on follow-ups, keeps the conversation's current mode. |
| `mcp_servers` | `Mapping[str, McpServerConfig]` | Inline MCP server definitions. Fully replaces creation-time servers for this run. |
| `local.force` | `bool` | Local agents only. Defaults to `False`. Expire a stuck active run before starting this message. Cloud returns `409 agent_busy` server-side, so no equivalent is needed. |
| `idempotency_key` | `str` | Optional client-generated idempotency key for the send. |
| `on_step` | `Callable[[ConversationStep], Any]` | Callback after each
[...]
conversation step (text, thinking, or tool batch). |
| `on_delta` | `Callable[[InteractionUpdate], Any]` | Callback per raw `InteractionUpdate`. |
[...]
Use `Agent.resume()` or `client.agents.resume()` to reattach to an existing agent by ID. Common flows: reconnecting to a long-running cloud agent that was kicked off earlier, or continuing a conversation after the local process restarted. Runtime is auto-detected from the ID prefix (`bc-` is cloud, anything else is local).
[...]

<result id="2">
<title>GustavoWinter/cursor-agent-orchestrator-mcp</title>
<url>https://github.com/GustavoWinter/cursor-agent-orchestrator-mcp</url>
<content>An MCP server that runs many `@cursor/sdk` agents in parallel from one Cursor chat — locally or on Cursor Cloud — with a **propose → confirm → execute** flow and one merged event stream.
[...]
> **TL;DR**: Add the MCP to `.cursor/mcp.json` and set `CURSOR_API_KEY`. Your agent gets tools like `propose_plan`, `confirm_plan`, `execute_plan`, and `stream_plan`. `CURSOR_ORCH_PROPOSE_PLAN_MODE` controls when `propose_plan` may run (`auto` asks yes/no first; `manual` only after you ask for orchestration). `confirm_plan` gates `execute_plan` unless `CURSOR_ORCH_SKIP_CONFIRMATION=true` is set before the MCP starts.
[...]
_plan`** — the planner reads your repos
[...]
runs a single SDK agent
[...]
diagram. It picks the right
[...]
1. **`confirm_plan`** — records your explicit approval. `execute_plan` is blocked until this is called (server-side, not just instructions).
2. **`execute_plan`** — spawns N independent SDK agents in parallel (locally or on Cursor Cloud). Each subagent gets the repo context, project conventions (`.cursor/rules/`, skills), and its own prompt slice.
3. **`stream_plan`** — merges all subagent event streams into one labeled feed so you see everything in one place.
[...]
| Tool | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `propose_plan` | Turn a goal into a JSON plan; does not run agents |
| `confirm_plan` | Record your approval of a plan |
| `execute_plan` | Spawn subagents and return a `planRunId` |
| `stream_plan` | Pull merged labeled events until `done` |
| `get_plan_status` | Snapshot run state + PR links |
| `get_subagent_result` | Per-label result text and PR URL |
| `cancel_plan` / `cancel_subagent` | Stop a run or one subagent |
| `prompt_one_shot` | Single SDK agent without the planner |
| `list_plans` | Recent plans and runs |
| `prune_runs` | Remove old finished runs |
| `attach_plan` | Re-attach to persisted run after restart (cancel only) |
| `resume_plan` | Fully resume a persisted plan run after MCP restart |</content>
</result>

[...]

<result id="4">
<title>@cursor/february</title>
<url>https://registry.npmjs.org/@cursor/february</url>
<content>TypeScript SDK for Cursor agents (private alpha — codenamed).
[...]
The primary API is the static `Agent` namespace. It creates durable agents, starts runs, streams normalized run events, waits for completion, resumes existing agents, and inspects persisted agent state.
[...]
- `run.stream()`: Async generator of normalized `SDKMessage` events.
- `run.wait()`: Resolves to a terminal `RunResult`.
- `run.cancel()`: Cancels a supported running run.
- `run.conversation()`: Returns accumulated `ConversationTurn[]` for this run.
- `run.supports(operation)`: Checks whether `stream`, `wait`, `cancel`, or `conversation` is supported.
- `run.unsupportedReason(operation)`: Explains why an operation is unavailable.
- `run.onDidChangeStatus(listener)`: Subscribes to run status changes.
[...]
`run.stream()` emits `SDKMessage` events. This is the stable public stream surface shared by local and cloud runs.
[...]
Live local runs can accumulate tool steps from raw executor deltas. Persisted replay reconstructs conversation from `SDKMessage` events, so replayed conversations can include tool-call steps when the persisted stream contains tool payload data.
[...]
`Agent.messages.list(...)` reads persisted conversation/checkpoint messages. It is complementary to `run.conversation()`, which is scoped to one run.
[...]
- Persisted conversation replay can only reconstruct information present in the public `SDKMessage` stream.
- Inline MCP server definitions are not persisted across `Agent.resume(...)`.
- Artifact download support is not implemented for local SDK agents yet.</content>
</result>

<result id="5">
<title>docs/development/cursor-backend.md at main · ralphkrauss/agent-orchestrator</title>
<url>https://github.com/ralphkrauss/agent-orchestrator/blob/main/docs/development/cursor-backend.md</url>
<content>```md
# Cursor Backend (Local SDK Runtime)
[...]
The `cursor` backend runs Cursor agents in-process through the
[`@cursor/sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cursor/sdk) TypeScript SDK.
[...]
Unlike the `codex` and `claude` backends, the cursor backend does not spawn a
CLI subprocess: it loads the SDK in the daemon Node process and streams typed
events.
[...]
This release ships the **local runtime only** (`local: { cwd }`). The agent
operates against files on disk in the run's `cwd`. Cloud and self-hosted
runtimes (`cloud: { repos, ... }`) are out of scope and tracked as follow-ups.
[...]
- subagents (`AgentDefinition`)
[...]
server forwarding from the
[...]
| Setting | Cursor behavior |
|---|---|
| `model` | Required. Pass a Cursor-side model id (e.g. `composer-2`). Resume calls thread per-run model overrides through `SendOptions.model`. |
| `reasoning_effort` | Rejected with `INVALID_INPUT`. The SDK exposes per-model parameter discovery (`Cursor.models.list()`) which is deferred. |
| `service_tier` | Rejected with `INVALID_INPUT`. |
| `worker_posture` | Optional; defaults to `'trusted'`. Under `'trusted'`, `Agent.create` and `Agent.resume` receive `local.settingSources: ['all']` so the SDK loads every ambient settings layer (project, user, team, MDM, plugins). Under `'restricted'`, the shim omits `settingSources` and the SDK falls back to its pre-#58 in-process defaults. |
[...]
## Sessions and resume
[...]
The runtime persists the durable Cursor `agentId` as the run's `session_id`.
Follow-ups call `Agent.resume(agentId, { apiKey, local: { cwd } })` and pass
the per-call `SendOptions.model` only when the follow-up changes the model.
[...]
A `Run.status === "cancelled"` is recorded with `runStatusOverride =
"cancelled"` so the run finalizes as cancelled rather than completed.</content>
</result>

## Executive summary

This workspace is a **greenfield scaffold**, not an existing orchestrator or SDK codebase. After a full file search and keyword scan, the only tracked source file is `README.md` (one line). There is **no** replay, recording, fixture, test, or mock infrastructure to analyze yet.

---

## 1. What is this project?

```
t3-orchestrator-v2-cursor-agent-sdk-record-proposed_plan-ggwFWW/
├── README.md          # sole source file

```

```1:1:README.md
# cursor-agent-sdk-record-proposed_plan
```

Git metadata suggests this was created by an automated test harness:

- Author: `T3 Code Test <t3code-test@example.com>`

### Inferred intent (from naming only)

The directory name `t3-orchestrator-v2-cursor-agent-sdk-record-proposed_plan-ggwFWW` strongly suggests a **planned feature task**, not an implemented repo:

| Name segment | Likely meaning |
|---|---|
| `t3-orchestrator-v2` | Second version of a T3 test orchestrator |
| `cursor-agent-sdk` | Integration with Cursor’s programmatic Agent SDK |
| `record-proposed_plan` | Record `proposed_plan` events/states for deterministic replay |
| `ggwFWW` | Ephemeral sandbox ID |

There is **no orchestrator code, no SDK wrapper, and no `proposed_plan` type** in this workspace.

---

## 2. Replay / recording / fixture infrastructure

### Keyword search results (entire repo)

| Keyword | Matches |
|---|---|
| `proposed_plan` | `README.md` title only |
| `record` | Substring of README title only |
| `replay` | **0** |
| `fixture` | **0** |
| `deterministic` | **0** |

| `mock` | **0** |
| `orchestrator` | **0** (only in path name) |
| `cursor-agent-sdk` | **0** (only in path/README title) |

**Conclusion:** No recording, replay, fixture, or persistence layer exists.

---

## 3. Test patterns, mocks, deterministic testing

**None present.** Specifically absent:

- No test runner config (`package.json`, `vitest.config.*`, `jest.config.*`, `pytest.ini`, etc.)
- No `__tests__/`, `test/`, or `*.test.*` / `*.spec.*` files
- No mock/stub utilities
- No snapshot or golden-file tests
- No VCR/cassette-style HTTP recording
- No seed/time/randomness controls for determinism

---

## 4. Project structure overview

| Path | Role |
|---|---|
| `README.md` | Project title placeholder |
| `.git/` | Initial commit with README only |
| *(everything else)* | **Does not exist** |

Expected structure for the task implied by the name (not yet present):

```
src/
  orchestrator/       # plan lifecycle, subagent coordination
  cursor-sdk/         # Agent.create, run.stream(), Agent.resume()
  record/             # event capture during propose_plan
  replay/             # fixture playback for tests
fixtures/
  proposed_plan/      # recorded plan JSON + SDK event streams
tests/
  replay/             # deterministic replay tests
```

---

## 5. Current gaps for deterministic replay fixtures

Because there is no code, **all** of the following are gaps:

1. **Event schema** — No type for `proposed_plan` or recorded SDK events (`SDKMessage`, tool calls, deltas).
2. **Recorder** — No hook on agent streaming (`run.stream()`, `on_delta`, `on_step`) to persist events.
3. **Fixture format** — No JSON/YAML convention for plans + event sequences + metadata (model, mode, cwd).
4. **Replayer** — No substitute transport that feeds recorded events instead of live SDK calls.
5. **Determinism controls** — No fixed clocks, idempotency keys, or stripped nondeterministic fields (timestamps, IDs).
6. **Test harness** — No assertions comparing replay output to expected snapshots.
7. **Orchestrator integration** — No `propose_plan` → `confirm_plan` → `execute_plan` flow to record.

---

## 6. Types/interfaces that would need extension

**None exist in this repo.** Based on the task name and typical Cursor SDK + orchestrator patterns, you would likely need:

```typescript
// Hypothetical — NOT in codebase

interface ProposedPlan {
  id: string;
  goal: string;
  subagents: SubagentSpec[];
  // ...
}

interface RecordedRun {
  mode: 'plan' | 'agent';
  agentId?: string;
  events: SDKMessage[];  // from run.stream()
  proposedPlan?: ProposedPlan;
  recordedAt: string;
}

interface ReplayFixture {
  name: string;
  input: { prompt: string; mode: 'plan' };
  recorded: RecordedRun;
  expected?: { finalStatus: string; planHash?: string };
}

interface RecordReplayTransport {
  record(run: AgentRun): AsyncIterable<SDKMessage>;
  replay(fixture: ReplayFixture): AsyncIterable<SDKMessage>;
}
```

Public Cursor SDK concepts relevant here (external, not in repo):

- `run.stream()` → normalized `SDKMessage` events (stable replay surface)
- `Agent.resume(agentId)` → reconnect to persisted agents
- `mode: "plan"` → plan-mode runs
- Persisted replay reconstructs conversation from `SDKMessage` streams

---

## 7. Suggested integration points (when code is added)

Since there is no code to anchor to, these are **architectural integration points** aligned with the task name:

### A. SDK stream boundary (primary record point)

Wrap `Agent.create()` / `run.stream()`:

```
Live:  prompt → Agent.create → run.stream() → [RECORDER] → consumer
Replay: fixture → [REPLAYER] → consumer (same interface as stream)
```

Record every `SDKMessage`; normalize timestamps/IDs before writing fixtures.

### B. Orchestrator plan lifecycle

If following a propose → confirm → execute model:

| Phase | Record | Replay |
|---|---|---|
| `propose_plan` | Full plan JSON + planning stream | Feed recorded plan stream; skip LLM |
| `confirm_plan` | Approval metadata | Inject fixed approval |
| `execute_plan` | Per-subagent streams | Replay subagent fixtures in parallel |

### C. Transport abstraction

```typescript
interface AgentTransport {
  create(options: CreateOptions): Promise<AgentHandle>;
  stream(handle: AgentHandle): AsyncIterable<SDKMessage>;
}

class LiveSdkTransport implements AgentTransport { /* @cursor/sdk */ }
class FixtureReplayTransport implements AgentTransport { /* read fixtures/ */ }
```

Tests inject `FixtureReplayTransport`; production uses `LiveSdkTransport`.

### D. Fixture layout (suggested)

```
fixtures/
  proposed_plan/
    simple-refactor/
      meta.json           # mode, model, cwd
      plan.json           # proposed plan artifact
      events.ndjson       # one SDKMessage per line
      expected.json       # optional assertions
```

### E. Determinism checklist

- Strip or normalize: `timestamp`, `runId`, `agentId`, token counts
- Use stable ordering for parallel subagent events (e.g. sort by `label` + `sequence`)
- Fixed `idempotency_key` on sends
- Mock filesystem/git side effects in replay mode

---

## 8. What you can do next

This workspace has nothing to extend yet. To proceed you would need either:

1. **The parent `t3-orchestrator-v2` repo** cloned or copied into this workspace, or  
2. **Agent mode** to scaffold the recorder/replayer/fixture layout from scratch.

If you have the orchestrator source elsewhere, point me at that repo (or switch to Agent mode with it checked out) and I can map concrete files, existing types, and exact hook points instead of this inferred architecture.

args.plan:
# Deterministic Replay Fixtures for `proposed_plan`

## Context

The workspace is a greenfield scaffold ([README.md](README.md)). This plan introduces the minimum structure to **record** plan-mode SDK streams and **replay** them as fixtures for deterministic tests.

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph live [Live mode]
    Prompt --> LiveTransport
    LiveTransport --> SDK["Cursor Agent SDK"]
    SDK --> Recorder
    Recorder --> Consumer
    Recorder --> FixtureFile
  end

  subgraph replay [Replay mode]
    FixtureFile2[Fixture file] --> ReplayTransport
    ReplayTransport --> Consumer2[Same consumer code]
  end
```

**Key idea:** Orchestrator code consumes `AsyncIterable<SDKMessage>` from a transport interface; production uses live SDK, tests use fixture replay.

## 1. Define core types

Add [`src/replay/types.ts`](src/replay/types.ts):

- `ProposedPlanFixture` — `{ name, input: { prompt, mode: "plan" }, events: SDKMessage[], plan?: unknown, meta: { model?, cwd? } }`
- `AgentTransport` — `{ create(opts), stream(handle): AsyncIterable<SDKMessage> }`
- `RecordOptions` / `ReplayOptions` — fixture path, normalize flag

Reuse SDK message shapes from `@cursor/agent-sdk` (or a thin local mirror) so fixtures stay aligned with `run.stream()` output.

## 2. Fixture format and layout

```
fixtures/proposed_plan/
  <case-name>/
    meta.json      # input prompt, mode, model, cwd
    events.ndjson  # one SDKMessage per line (post-normalization)
    plan.json      # extracted proposed plan artifact (optional convenience)
    expected.json  # optional assertions (status, plan hash, key fields)
```

- NDJSON keeps diffs readable and supports streaming replay.
- Commit normalized fixtures only (no secrets, no absolute paths).

## 3. Normalization for determinism

Add [`src/replay/normalize.ts`](src/replay/normalize.ts) to strip or replace volatile fields before write/compare:

- Timestamps, run/agent IDs, token counts, latency fields
- Sort parallel sub-events by stable key (`type` + `sequence` or `label`)
- Replace cwd/home with placeholders (e.g. `$WORKSPACE`)

Apply normalization in both **recorder** (on write) and **test assertions** (on compare).

## 4. Implement recorder

Add [`src/replay/recorder.ts`](src/replay/recorder.ts):

- Wrap live `AgentTransport.stream()` and tee events to consumer + buffer
- On stream end, extract `proposed_plan` payload from terminal plan message(s)
- Write fixture directory via `writeFixture(name, data)`

CLI entrypoint [`src/cli/record-fixture.ts`](src/cli/record-fixture.ts):

```bash
pnpm record-fixture --name simple-refactor --prompt "..." --mode plan
```

## 5. Implement replay transport

Add [`src/replay/replay-transport.ts`](src/replay/replay-transport.ts):

- Load fixture by name/path
- Yield `events.ndjson` lines as `SDKMessage` with configurable tick delay (default 0)
- Implement `AgentTransport` so orchestrator plan flow is unchanged

Wire via env or factory in [`src/transport/index.ts`](src/transport/index.ts):

```typescript
export function createTransport(): AgentTransport {
  return process.env.REPLAY_FIXTURE
    ? new ReplayTransport(process.env.REPLAY_FIXTURE)
    : new LiveSdkTransport();
}
```

## 6. Orchestrator hook: `proposed_plan`

Add [`src/orchestrator/propose-plan.ts`](src/orchestrator/propose-plan.ts):

1. `createTransport()` → `create({ mode: "plan", prompt })`
2. Consume stream until plan-complete message
3. Return parsed plan + raw events (for optional inline recording in dev)

This is the single integration point both live and replay modes share.

## 7. Deterministic tests

Add [`tests/replay/proposed-plan.test.ts`](tests/replay/proposed-plan.test.ts):

- Set `REPLAY_FIXTURE=fixtures/proposed_plan/simple-refactor`
- Run `proposePlan(prompt)` and assert:
  - Stable plan hash or snapshot of normalized `plan.json`
  - Expected status from `expected.json`
- Add one golden fixture recorded from a real run, then lock behavior

Use Vitest (or project-standard runner) with `fixtures/` as test data.

## 8. Deliverables (minimal scope)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/replay/types.ts` | Shared fixture + transport types |
| `src/replay/normalize.ts` | Deterministic field stripping |
| `src/replay/recorder.ts` | Live capture → fixture |
| `src/replay/replay-transport.ts` | Fixture → stream |
| `src/transport/live-sdk-transport.ts` | Thin SDK wrapper |
| `src/orchestrator/propose-plan.ts` | Plan-mode orchestration |
| `src/cli/record-fixture.ts` | One-shot recording CLI |
| `fixtures/proposed_plan/simple-refactor/` | Seed fixture |
| `tests/replay/proposed-plan.test.ts` | Replay regression test |

## Success criteria

- Same orchestrator code path works in live and replay modes.
- Re-running replay tests produces identical normalized plan output across machines.
- New fixtures can be recorded with one CLI command and committed as NDJSON + JSON.

## 8. Deliverables (minimal scope)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
